verses.1: A Psalm of Asaph. God certainly is good to Israel, To those who are pure in heart!
verses.2: But as for me, my feet came close to stumbling, My steps had almost slipped.
verses.3: For I was envious of the arrogant As I saw the prosperity of the wicked.
verses.4: For there are no pains in their death, And their belly is fat.
verses.5: They are not in trouble like other people, Nor are they tormented together with the rest of mankind.
verses.6: Therefore arrogance is their necklace; The garment of violence covers them.
verses.7: Their eye bulges from fatness; The imaginations of their heart overflow.
verses.8: They mock and wickedly speak of oppression; They speak from on high.
verses.9: They have set their mouth against the heavens, And their tongue parades through the earth.
verses.10: Therefore his people return here, And abundant waters are drunk by them.
verses.11: They say, “How does God know? And is there knowledge with the Most High?”
verses.12: Behold, these are the wicked; And always at ease, they have increased in wealth.
verses.13: Surely in vain I have kept my heart pure And washed my hands in innocence;
verses.14: For I have been stricken all day long, And punished every morning.
verses.15: If I had said, “I will speak this way,” Behold, I would have betrayed the generation of Your children.
verses.16: When I thought of understanding this, It was troublesome in my sight
verses.17: Until I entered the sanctuary of God; Then I perceived their end.
verses.18: You indeed put them on slippery ground; You dropped them into ruin.
verses.19: How they are destroyed in a moment! They are utterly swept away by sudden terrors!
verses.20: Like a dream when one awakes, Lord, when stirred, You will despise their image.
verses.21: When my heart was embittered And I was pierced within,
verses.22: Then I was stupid and ignorant; I was like an animal before You.
verses.23: Nevertheless I am continually with You; You have taken hold of my right hand.
verses.24: You will guide me with Your plan, And afterward receive me to glory.
verses.25: Whom do I have in heaven but You? And with You, I desire nothing on earth.
verses.26: My flesh and my heart may fail, But God is the strength of my heart and my portion forever.
verses.27: For, behold, those who are far from You will perish; You have destroyed all those who are unfaithful to You.
verses.28: But as for me, the nearness of God is good for me; I have made the Lord GOD my refuge, So that I may tell of all Your works.
